LAW AND MOTION 302, VERIFIED APPLICATION OF ELIZABETH CRABTREE TO APPEAR AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FOR THE DEFENDANTS AMEC ENVIRONMENT & INFRASTRUCTURE, INC., MACTEC ENGINEERING AND CONSULTING, INC., DONALD QUIGLEY AND CHRIS COUTU IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE APPLICATION CONFORMS WITH CRC 9.40. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: JOHN STEWART; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 =302/JS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMEC ENVIRONMENT & INFRASTRUCTURE, INC., MACTEC ENGINEERING AND CONSULTING, INC., DONALD QUIGLEY AND CHRIS COUTU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS INTRODUCED EVIDENCE RAISING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CLAIM PROCESS, AS CONTEMPLATED IN PARAGRAPH 13, WAS COMMENCED WITHIN ONE YEAR OF DISCOVERY OF THE ALLEGEDLY DIFFERENT SERVICES, AND THAT EVIDENCE INCLUDES THE FOLLOWING: THE SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF JAN GRUEN, EXHIBIT A, THAT STATES THE REPAIRS ARE NEEDED AT THE PROJECT AND PLAINTIFF WANTS TO MEET WITH AMEC TO TRY TO RESOLVE THE ISSUES OF REPAIR COSTS AMICABLY. FURTHER EVIDENCE IS FOUND AT THE REPLY DECLARATION OF DONALD QUIGLEY AT EXHIBIT C, A SEPTEMBER 25, 2013, LETTER FROM AMEC'S COUNSEL TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHOWING THAT AMEC WAS APPRISED OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST IT, AND EXHIBIT D, A SEPTEMBER 19, 2013, LETTER FROM PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. THE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM

DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A SUR-REPLY, NO MORE THAN 5 PAGES IN LENGTH, UP TO 5 DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE NEW HEARING DATE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendants' Amec Environment Infrastructure, Inc., Mactec Engineering Consulting, Inc., Donald W. Quigley And Chris Coutu
Off Calendar Sep-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAN GRUEN OF THE SSL LAW FIRM LLP, TEL. NO. 814-6400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHAD V. THERIOT OF KILPATRICK TOWNSEND LLP, TEL. NO. (404)815-6211 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AMEC/MACTEC, QUIGLY CONSTRUCTION ET AL.;
ATTY. RYAN MAU, TEL. NO. 296-7700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CAMPBELL CONSTRUCTION INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court denied the motion to strike the Supplemental Gruen Declaration. DEFENDANTS AMEC ENVIRONMENT & INFRASTRUCTURE, INC., MACTEC ENGINEERING AND CONSULTING, INC., DONALD QUIGLEY AND CHRIS COUTU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff has introduced evidence raising a triable issue of material fact as to whether the claim process, as contemplated in paragraph 13, was commenced within one year of discovery of the allegedly different services, and that evidence includes the following: the supplemental declaration of Jan Gruen, Exhibit A, that states the repairs are needed at the project and plaintiff wants to meet with AMEC to try to resolve the issues of repair costs amicably. Further evidence is found at the reply declaration of Donald Quigley at Exhibit C, a September 25, 2013, letter from AMEC's counsel to plaintiff's counsel showing that AMEC was apprised of plaintiff's claims against it, and Exhibit D, a September 19, 2013, letter from plaintiff's counsel to defendant's counsel. The plaintiff's request for attorney's fees is denied without prejudice. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order to comply with CCP 437 C(G) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendants' Amec Environment Infrastructure, Inc., Mactec Engineering Consulting, Inc., Donald W. Quigley And Chris Coutu
Off Calendar Aug-29-2014 Continued To Sep-17-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS AMEC ENVIRONMENT & INFRASTRUCTURE, INC., MACTEC ENGINEERING AND CONSULTING, INC., DONALD QUIGLEY AND CHRIS COUTU's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to September 17, 2014. On August 27, 2014, Plaintiff filed an Opposition to Defendants' Motion to Strike, making substantive arguments that respond to arguments raised in Defendants' reply brief to the Motion for Summary Judgment. Defendants may file a sur-reply, no more than 5 pages in length, up to 5 days in advance of the new hearing date. The Court will consider all papers filed thus far, including the supplemental declaration. Judge: John Stewart, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/JKS)
